description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who has finished cleaning a small image-classification
dataset located under /home/user/datasets.  
Before starting model training you want to:

1. Create a versioned backup of the whole datasets directory as a single compressed
   GNU–tar archive called
      /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_v1.tar.gz

2. Produce a simple, human-readable manifest that allows you (and the automated
   grader) to verify what got archived.  
   The manifest must be saved as
      /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_manifest.log

3. The manifest must have **exactly four** non-empty lines – one for every data file
   contained in the dataset – with the following format (one space between the two
   columns, no extra spaces, no trailing slash):

      <relative_path_inside_archive> <size_in_bytes>

   Example of the required layout (do **NOT** add the header line):
      images/img1.jpg 18  
      images/img2.jpg 18  
      labels/img1.txt 18  
      labels/img2.txt 18

   • “relative_path_inside_archive” is the path **as stored in the tarball**, counted
     from its root (i.e. “datasets/” must be omitted).  
   • “size_in_bytes” is the exact byte size of the file on disk (Hint: use
     ‘stat -c%s’).

4. Make sure the /home/user/backups directory exists before writing the two files
   there.

5. Nothing else should be printed to the manifest – no extra blank lines, headers,
   hashes or timestamps.

Success criteria that the automatic tests will check:

• /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_v1.tar.gz exists, is a valid gzip-compressed
  tar file and contains the four expected data files underneath a single top-level
  directory named “datasets”.  
• /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_manifest.log exists with exactly four
  lines that match the format given above and whose size values are correct.  
• The names in the manifest correspond exactly (character-for-character) to the
  file paths stored in the archive after the “datasets/” prefix is stripped.

Use any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities you prefer.  
No root privileges are available.
truth:
Initial dataset that already exists on disk before the task starts:

/home/user/datasets/images/img1.jpg      → ascii text: "fake image data 1\n"     (18 bytes)
/home/user/datasets/images/img2.jpg      → ascii text: "fake image data 2\n"     (18 bytes)
/home/user/datasets/labels/img1.txt      → ascii text: "label for image 1\n"     (18 bytes)
/home/user/datasets/labels/img2.txt      → ascii text: "label for image 2\n"     (18 bytes)

All four files have permissions 644 and are owned by user:user.

The tests will:

1. Verify that /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_v1.tar.gz exists and can be
   unpacked with ‘tar -xzf’.
2. After extraction, confirm that exactly one top-level directory called “datasets”
   appears and that, relative to that directory, the four files listed above are
   present and byte-identical to the originals.
3. Check that /home/user/backups/training_data_backup_manifest.log exists,
   contains exactly four newline-separated, non-empty lines and that each line
   matches one of the following strings **exactly**:

   images/img1.jpg 18
   images/img2.jpg 18
   labels/img1.txt 18
   labels/img2.txt 18

   (ordering inside the manifest is not evaluated).
